Tool Call: retrieve("construction of square knot")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on the gathered evidence regarding the construction methods of the square knot and reviews of related surgical techniques, further confirmation supports our previous decision. The square knot indeed comprises two single loops tied in opposite directions. While some documents discuss more complex types of ligature formations, none describe the square knot differently than what was previously established — two single loops oriented oppositely to achieve proper binding. Other examples highlight varied applications requiring differing techniques, reinforcing rather than contradicting the simplicity and specificity inherent to creating a standard square knot correctly. Thus, I continue agreeing with Option A as accurate due to consistent adherence to foundational principles outlined even amidst diverse surgical practices referenced extensively across reviewed literature.
